picks.title: Alicja Kwade: Waiting Pavilions
picks.blurb: Mirror-and-stone pavilions scattered across the Victorian prison yard, bending one courtyard into several possible ones. The rare public installation that rewards standing still.

picks.title: The Body Transformed: Global Jewellery from the Met
picks.venue: Hong Kong Palace Museum, West Kowloon
picks.blurb: Five millennia of ornament from the Met — Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas — read as one continuous argument: the body was always the first gallery.

picks.title: Fermata: Hong Kong in Venice
picks.venue: Hong Kong Museum of Art, Tsim Sha Tsui
picks.blurb: The city's Venice Biennale presentation, restaged at home. What Hong Kong chose to say about itself to Europe, now answering to its toughest audience — locals.

more.title: Ancient Egypt Unveiled: Treasures from Egyptian Museums
Better Together — Partnering Up for Hong Kong Movies
